reasoning_content: We are asked: "Is 263 a prime number? Answer yes or no." So we need to determine if 263 is prime. Let's check divisibility. It's odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Ends with 3, not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so no. 11: 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so no. 13: 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so no. 17: 17*15=255, 17*16=272, so no. 19: 19*13=247, 19*14=266, so no. 23: 23*11=253, 23*12=276, so no. 29: 29*9=261, 29*10=290, so no. 31: 31*8=248, 31*9=279, so no. 37: 37*7=259, 37*8=296, so no. 41: 41*6=246, 41*7=287, so no. 43: 43*6=258, 43*7=301, so no. 47: 47*5=235, 47*6=282, so no. 53: 53*4=212, 53*5=265, so no. We only need to test up to sqrt(263) which is about 16.2, so actually we only need to test primes up to 13? Wait sqrt(263) ≈ 16.2, so primes up to 13: 2,3,5,7,11,13. We've tested all those. 13: 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so 263 not divisible by 13. So indeed 263 is prime. Answer yes.
